text: For many years, the practice activities have been an important pedagogical tool for enhancing the teaching and learning process. In addition to stimulating the interest of students for the area of science, the practical activities enable the construction of knowledge and contribute to the development of a critical view of the world. However, the lectures remain the most used educational strategy. This study aimed to evaluate, recognize and strengthen the practical science teaching in two schools located in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE: a rural school, Duque de Caxias, and an urban school, August 3. The information was collected in visits to the schools and through the application of questionnaires with teachers. The Duque de Caxias school has better infrastructure and a greater involvement of teachers in the realization of practical classes than the August 3 school. Both schools have listed several obstacles to the realization of experimental classes in the classrooms, such as lack of materials, large number of students and lack of time for the preparation of classes. To contribute to the strengthening of the practical activities in the schools, laboratory manuals specialized for the 6th to 9th grades were developed.
